action: Climbed to the eastern cliff before sunrise and sat there alone, watching the horizon.
thought: Woke at five. Couldn't sleep anyway.

action: Started laying the foundation for a second room in his house, just in case.
thought: Might as well make room. Someone could use it.

action: Slept in until 9am, then walked barefoot down to the shore for a swim.
thought: Salt water, quiet beach, no notifications. This is fine.

description: Ryan spotted Marcus on the cliff and waved up at him. Marcus gave a single nod and looked back at the sea.
dialogue: Come down later — I'm cooking dinner. There's enough for everyone.

action: Gathered driftwood pieces and arranged them near his tent, working methodically without hurrying.
thought: They're all doing their thing. The work keeps the mind quiet.

action: Walked down to the lagoon searching for fresh water and spotted Jade wading near the shallows.
thought: She's over here too. We could actually solve the water problem together.

action: Drifted along the lagoon edge, letting the cool water run over her arms and testing the reef beneath.
thought: The island doesn't care what we do here. That's what makes it feel clean.

description: Ryan pointed out a freshwater stream trickling down the rocks. Jade smiled and said they should both bring water back together.
dialogue: Hey, you see that stream? We're actually pretty well set up on this island.

action: Sits at the edge of his tent, listening to the island settle into darkness.
thought: The work kept the noise down. Now it's back.

action: Lies in his house, turning over the day's moments with Jade, already thinking about tomorrow.
thought: She's genuine. Easy to be around. Don't push it.

action: Settles into her hammock as the stars appear above the lagoon.
thought: No rush, no angle. Just present. That's rare.

description: As night fell, Ryan walked Jade back toward her hammock and told her they made a solid team on the first day.
dialogue: We make a decent team, you know.

action: Left his tent and walked to the rocks by the eastern shore, where the water was loudest.
thought: The quiet hurts more than I thought.

action: Lay in the darkness of his house, replaying the day with Jade over and over.
thought: I'm already falling for her. That's the problem.

action: Walked down the beach at midnight, the way she always does when sleep feels too heavy.
thought: The dark makes me feel less alone.

description: Marcus and Jade found each other on the dark beach—a moment of unexpected stillness between two people who don't usually stop moving.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: Never tried. Nights feel better awake.

dialogue.speaker: jade
dialogue.line: It was. Might not be now.
